articles.title: Telangana government declares Rs 4 lakh ex-gratia for 16 heatstroke deaths - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
The Telangana Government has announced a Rs 4 lakh ex-gratia payment to the families of 16 individuals who have died due to heatstroke amid an intensifying heat wave. Telangana Minister Ponguleti Srinivas Reddy convened an emergency review meeting with senior officials at the Secretariat on Saturday to address the worsening heat conditions affecting seven districts.
With temperatures soaring unusually high and the India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasting intensified heat wave conditions over the next three days, district collectors have been instructed to stay vigilant and promptly implement relief measures. The fatalities include four in Jayashankar Bhupalpally, three each in Warangal Urban, Karimnagar, and Nizamabad, and one each in Jogulamba Gadwal, Rangareddy, and Suryapet.
Officials have been directed to ensure the availability of cold drinking water at public locations, distribute ORS packets and buttermilk, and maintain constant readiness of emergency medical services. Additionally, authorities are mandated to issue hourly public awareness alerts through mainstream media and social platforms.
Special precautions have been advised for vulnerable groups such as senior citizens, pregnant women, children, and those with pre-existing medical conditions. The general public is urged to avoid going outdoors between 11 AM and 4 PM unless absolutely necessary.
Minister Reddy also called for dedicated drinking water arrangements for birds and animals in both villages and towns to address environmental distress.
The IMD has issued a severe heat wave warning, effective until May 26, for districts including Hyderabad, Rangareddy, Karimnagar, Khammam, Nalgonda, Suryapet, Mulugu, and Mahabubnagar.
